exercises.title: Vowel Sound Identification
exercises.description: Read these words and circle the vowel sound: cat, bed, sit, hot, cup, cake, these, bike, home, cute. Group them by short vs long vowel sounds.
hints: Remember: short vowels are quick sounds, long vowels say their letter name
Group by the vowel letter: a, e, i, o, u

solution.code:
Short: cat(a), bed(e), sit(i), hot(o), cup(u)
Long: cake(a), these(e), bike(i), home(o), cute(u)

exercises.title: Minimal Pairs Practice
exercises.description: Listen to and practice these minimal pairs. Write the difference you hear: ship/sheep, bed/bad, full/fool, light/right, think/sink
hints: Focus on the vowel sound difference
Ship has /ɪ/ (short i), sheep has /iː/ (long e)

solution.code:
ship /ɪ/ vs sheep /iː/
bed /e/ vs bad /æ/
full /ʊ/ vs fool /uː/
light /aɪ/ vs right /aɪ/ (consonant difference)
think /θ/ vs sink /s/ (consonant difference)

exercises.title: Syllable Counting
exercises.description: Count the syllables in: university, beautiful, understand, computer, communicate, interesting, comfortable
hints: Clap as you say each syllable
Listen for each distinct vowel sound

solution.code:
university = u-ni-ver-si-ty (6)
beautiful = beau-ti-ful (3)
understand = un-der-stand (3)
computer = com-pu-ter (3)
communicate = com-mu-ni-cate (4)
interesting = in-ter-est-ing (4)
comfortable = com-for-ta-ble (4)

exercises.title: Tongue Twister Challenge
exercises.description: Practice these tongue twisters 5 times each. Focus on clear pronunciation, not speed.
hints: Start slowly and increase speed gradually
Focus on the difficult consonant clusters

solution.code:
1. She sells seashells by the seashore.
2. Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.
3. How much wood would a woodchuck chuck?
4. Red lorry, yellow lorry, red lorry, yellow lorry.
5. The sixth sick sheikh's sixth sheep's sick.
